result:
Charles Darwin finished writing "The Expression of the Emotions in Man and Animals" in 1872. He began writing it on January 17, 1871, two days after completing "The Descent of Man," and finished most of the text within four months. He completed work on the proofs by August 22, 1872, and the book was published on November 26, 1872.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Expression_of_the_Emotions_in_Man_and_Animals), [2](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2781895/), [3](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10426026/)

He finished writing "The Formation of Vegetable Mould through the Action of Worms" in 1881; it was published in October of that year.[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Formation_of_Vegetable_Mould_Through_the_Action_of_Worms) This was his last scientific book, and it went on sale shortly before his death in April 1882.[5](https://darwin200.christs.cam.ac.uk/worms), [6](https://www.nypl.org/blog/2012/04/19/earthworms-darwins-last-manuscript)

While "The Formation of Vegetable Mould through the Action of Worms" was his last scientific book published during his lifetime, Darwin did write his autobiography, which was published posthumously in 1887, edited by his son Francis Darwin.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Darwin_bibliography), [8](https://darwin-online.org.uk/EditorialIntroductions/vanWyhe_Manuscripts.html) He also made corrections for a later edition of "The Formation of Vegetable Mould through the Action of Worms" in 1882.[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Darwin_bibliography)
